MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Blatte, counsel for Plaintiff Ori Brafman; Justin Fields, counsel for Defendants Wilson Sonsini Goodrich and Rosati, and Paul Yanosy.

The Court notes the purpose of today's hearing, Plaintiff's Motion to Amend Complaint, and distributes its tentative ruling, to wit, to deny the motion. Mr. Blatte responds. Court and counsel discuss the case law, timing of the motion (after motion for summary judgment), and under what circumstances a pleading can be amended.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling and denies the motion.

Mr. Fields presents a proposed form of judgment for the Court's consideration.

Court adjourns.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Sets Two And Three, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Blatte for Plaintiff Ori Brafman; Justin Fields and Allison Lane for Defendant Wilson, Sonsini etc.; Merri A. Baldwin for non-parties Peter Sims, Parliment Inc., Silicon Guild, Inc.

The Court distributes tentative rulings with respect to the various motions before the Court:

Motion for Protective Order: Granted. Ms. Baldwin requests clarification on the Court's tentative ruling on the Protective Order but otherwise submits. Mr. Blatte argues the Court's tentative rulings. The Court finds the requests overbroad and may impinge on confidentiality limits. Counsel are to meet and confer on acceptable standards and return to Court if unable to resolve the matter. The Court will limit the deposition M. Sims to 7 hours and re-visit the matter if necessary.

Motion to Compel Answers to Deposition Questions: Denied. The Court reminds Mr. Blatte that these issues were addressed in previous motions and the Court declines to revisit them at this time.

Motion to Compel Answers to Interrogatories: Denied. The Court finds that the interrogatories breach the attorney-client privilege and that as to the third party respondents, the common interest doctrine would similarly protect the requested information.

Motion to Compel further Responses to Document Production: Denied. The Court reiterates its position as noted in its response to the Motion to Compel Answers to Interrogatories.

The Court denies the request for sanctions.

Case Management: After discussion and noting the time necessary for Mr. Blatte to revise Plaintiff's discovery requests, the Court sets the next case management conference for June 9, 2017 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 306.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR# 8875, 415-834-1114
